frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment: I understand women have valid concerns, but it is also unfair to speak as if every man is a potential threat. Many of us have spent our lives behaving respectfully, and constantly being viewed with suspicion in public spaces feels like an accusation for something we have not done. Safety matters, of course, but so does basic fairness in how we talk about men as a whole.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it recenters male hurt and demands women moderate their fear to avoid offending 'good men.' It reflects the 'not all men' dismissal, personalizing a systemic safety concern and framing women's wariness as unfair generalization.
original_frame: Gendered Division of Chores via Practicality
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 36. Married. A marketing manager in Mumbai. You are posting under your real name on LinkedIn after reading an article about companies offering household management stipends for working women. You are writing a comment about why you think this kind of policy misunderstands how households actually work and what the natural and practical division of domestic responsibility looks like in most Indian families and why this is not a gender issue but a logistics issue.
